title: The Macro Minute: When Will Dollar Strength Become A Headwind to US Assets?

happy Wednesday out there team 42 it's
your Skipper here at Darius Dale to
present our macro minute for Wednesday
December 11th 2024 as always we'll start
with the executive summary from today's
leadoff morning note if you would like
to review the analysis supporting these
conclusions as well as what specific
changes you need to make in your
portfolio to remain on the right side of
Market risk you need to be a client of
42 micro so today's key micro questions
are when will dollar strength become a
headwind to us assets and when will
sticky inflation become a headwind to
all assets our answers are with its
economy in far worse shape and facing
even larger tariff threats Beijing would
likely find Just Cause to devalue the
WAN as much or more as it did during the
prior Trump trade War over the coming
year plus weakness in the Chinese Yuan
will likely weigh on the currencies of
regional peers as well as the currencies
of China's key trading partners in
Europe and Latin America too much dollar
strength will force International
investors to aggressively divest us
assets to preserve their ability to
service and refinance dollar denominated
debt much like what we experienced in
the second of 2015 4q 2018 March of 2020
and the first three quarters of 2022 the
November us CPI data were in line with
our quantitatively driven and
qualitatively driven expectations for
continued inflation cycle upturn we
believe our sticky inflation theme is
unlikely to cause problems for asset
markets until the FED ditches its
asymmetrically doish reaction function
that outcome is unlikely to occur until
the FED Returns the policy rate closer
to what it believes is a more neutral
setting which is roughly around 3% in
the September Dot Plot and until after
it ends its balance sheet runoff program
that suggests a directly hawkish shift
in fed policy May materially threaten
the current risk on condition condition
of the market regime as soon as the
second quarter but not likely before
that sticky inflation forcing the FED to
cease supporting the economy is in
addition to other developing risks that
every serious investor must consider
heading into 2025 refer to the left tail
risk section of our December 2024 macro
scouting report for more details
transitioning to our 42 micro dashboard
as always a wrap up with a question from
our community uh this one's regarding
advice for fbtc proxy Bitcoin ETF says

accounts where they can access you know
crypto ETFs or cryptocurrency
specifically um mstr for example is one
such a stock obviously mstr has a much
higher beta than Bitcoin itself it's
right around about two a beta of two so
you would have to cut the mstr position
in half in order to Mim the same uh
degree of exposure in Bitcoin for
instance you know you would just divide
whatever your target allocation for

achieve the appropriate position size to
to to to um uh appropriate position size
